From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

For the IndyCar Series event, see [IndyCar Grand Prix](/wiki/IndyCar_Grand_Prix "IndyCar Grand Prix").

Motor race

The **Indianapolis motorcycle Grand Prix** was a motorcycling event held on the [combined road course](/wiki/Oval_track_racing#Combined_road_course "Oval track racing") at the [Indianapolis Motor Speedway](/wiki/Indianapolis_Motor_Speedway "Indianapolis Motor Speedway") in [Speedway, Indiana](/wiki/Speedway,_Indiana "Speedway, Indiana"). From 2008 to 2015, it was held as part of the [Grand Prix motorcycle racing](/wiki/Grand_Prix_motorcycle_racing "Grand Prix motorcycle racing") season (MotoGP). The event was revived in 2020 with [MotoAmerica](/wiki/MotoAmerica "MotoAmerica").

The addition of a motorcycle race at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway loosely coincided with the track's [Centennial Era](/wiki/Indianapolis_500#Centennial_Era "Indianapolis 500"), which celebrated the 100th anniversary of the opening of the track (1909) and the 100th anniversary of the [first 500](/wiki/1911_Indianapolis_500 "1911 Indianapolis 500") (1911). The motorcycle race represented a "throwback" to the early days of the Speedway, as motorcycle races were in fact the first competitive racing events held on the track in August 1909.

## Track layout

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Track layout")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/12/Indianapolis_Moto_GP.svg/250px-Indianapolis_Moto_GP.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Indianapolis_Moto_GP.svg)

Original Motorcycle Circuit layout (2008–2013)

The circuit used is a modified version of the [combined road course](/wiki/Oval_track_racing#Combined_road_course "Oval track racing") used for the [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One") [United States Grand Prix](/wiki/United_States_Grand_Prix "United States Grand Prix") from 2000 to 2007. The circuit was reversed to a counterclockwise orientation, and a new complex of corners was added inside the infield of oval turn one. The new "Snake Pit" section, as it became known, was nicknamed for its location where notorious revelry once took place during the 1970s. The new series of corners precluded the bikes from using any of the banked oval corners. The double-hairpin near the [museum](/wiki/Indianapolis_Motor_Speedway_Museum "Indianapolis Motor Speedway Museum") and before the Hulman Straight was replaced with more traditional esses. Furthermore, the motorcycles stay low in the south short chute (approaching the Snake Pit section) between oval turn 1 and 2, using the pavement of the warm-up lane rather than the oval track's short chute.

The circuit was re-profiled in 2014, with three new motorcycle-only sections: one in the Snake Pit (which has become a prime overtaking spot), a more open entrance to Hulman Straight, and the final esses leading to the pit straight was changed to be more open and flowing.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The inaugural Red Bull Indianapolis GP took place 99 years after the previous motorcycle race at the track, in 1909.

### National Motorcycle Race Meet (1909)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: National Motorcycle Race Meet (1909)")]

The first motorsports event at the track consisted of 7 motorcycle races, ranging from 1 to 10 miles in length, on August 14, 1909, on the 2.5 mile oval.[[2]](#cite_note-scott-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) All races were sanctioned by the Federation of American Motorcyclists (FAM). A planned feature race of 25 miles was canceled, as was a proposed second day of competition, due to concerns over suitability of track surface for motorcycle use.

### Motorcycles on Meridian rally

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Motorcycles on Meridian rally")]

The race became part of a major motorcycle event organized by Indianapolis Motor Speedway, the Motorcycles on Meridian [motorcycle rally](/wiki/Motorcycle_rally "Motorcycle rally"), held on [Meridian Street](/wiki/Meridian_Street_(Indianapolis) "Meridian Street (Indianapolis)") near [Monument Circle](/wiki/Monument_Circle "Monument Circle") in downtown Indianapolis, attracting approximately 10,000 cyclists each night.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Various dirt tracks held flat-track motorcycle races during the weekend which became analogous to [midget car racing](/wiki/Midget_car_racing "Midget car racing")'s "[Night Before the 500](/wiki/Night_Before_the_500 "Night Before the 500")" held during the Indianapolis 500, often drawing star MotoGP riders in attendance. The event continued to be held, and with the [2020 Indianapolis 500](/wiki/2020_Indianapolis_500 "2020 Indianapolis 500") being moved to the August event of the Motorcycles on Meridian date, both the Motorcycles on Meridian and the [MotoAmerica](/wiki/MotoAmerica "MotoAmerica") round were changed.

## Support races

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Support races")]

### Red Bull Rookies Cup (2008)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Red Bull Rookies Cup (2008)")]

The Red Bull AMA Rookies Cup was an entry-level series for young riders, held as support features primarily at [AMA Superbike](/wiki/AMA_Superbike "AMA Superbike") events. It was the US counterpart to the Red Bull MotoGP Rookies Cup, a similar series held as support features at European [MotoGP](/wiki/Grand_Prix_motorcycle_racing "Grand Prix motorcycle racing") events. The Red Bull Riders Cup was an all-star event combining the leading riders in the AMA Rookies Cup and MotoGP Rookies Cup series. The Red Bull AMA Rookies Cup and Red Bull Riders Cup each held a race, as support to the Red Bull Indianapolis GP. All participating motorcycles: [KTM](/wiki/KTM "KTM").

## MotoAmerica

### MotoAmerica Superbike and Superstock 1000 (2015, 2020)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indianapolis_motorcycle_Grand_Prix&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: MotoAmerica Superbike and Superstock 1000 (2015, 2020)")]

The [MotoAmerica](/wiki/Motoamerica "Motoamerica") Superbike Championship ran two races, each with two classes being run concurrently, as support to the Red Bull Indianapolis GP. The Superstock 1000 class was abolished after 2017. In 2020, the series was originally scheduled to return as part of Motorcycles on Meridian. Because of the Indianapolis 500 being rescheduled for the Meridian weekend, the races occurred in mid-October with limited number of spectators.
